# The AI Marketing Agent Summit | Trainer Brief & Session Flow

<callout icon="🎯" color="blue_bg">
	**AI MARKETING AGENT SUMMIT** | Wed Jul 8, 2026 | **Duration:** 3 Hours (11:00pm to 2:00am SGT) | **Format:** Live Builds | **Host:** Matt | **Experts:** TBC (recruiting)
</callout>

---

## What This Event Is

A 3-hour live event. Not a lecture. Three experts each build a different AI marketing agent in real time, and the challenge winners are announced live. The promise: members leave seeing exactly what is possible next, with their own agent better than when they walked in.

### Why This Matters

These members just spent 15 days building their first AI marketing agent. They set up their business, their customer, and their voice. They built an idea engine. They graduated.

The Summit is where "my first agent" becomes "I can build anything." If they leave inspired and with a sharper agent, the challenge was the start of something real. If they leave flat, it was just homework.

**Your job as host and experts is simple: every member should leave with at least one new idea they can add to their own agent, and the winners should feel like stars.**

---

## The Audience

Hundreds of NAS.com members. Founders, solopreneurs, marketers. Most are not technical. They just built their first agent in Claude over 15 days. Design everything for the person in the middle. Beginners need to follow along. Keep it accessible: build live, narrate every step, no code, no jargon.

**Assume no one is a developer.** Every person built one simple agent and is here to see what is next.

---

Every expert builds one different agent live, so members leave thinking "I can build that too." Full briefs for each expert: see Summit Guest-Experts.

	**For members who did the 15-day challenge:** you already have your agent (your Project, your business, your customer, your voice). Tonight, keep it open and improve it as you watch. Steal one idea from each expert.
	**For anyone joining fresh:** welcome. You will not build a full agent tonight, but you will leave with three clear blueprints and a free path to build your own (the challenge runs again).
</callout>

## Session Flow (3 Hours)

<callout icon="🎬" color="blue_bg">
	**Key Rule:** never go more than 35 minutes without a state change. Each expert builds live and narrates every step. Build WITH the audience, not AT them. The live builds are the wow. Protect them.
	Start time is 11:00pm SGT. Times below are relative to the start (0:00). Once the start time is locked in Source of Truth, every segment maps to a clock time inside the 3-hour window.
</callout>

<table fit-page-width="true" header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg">
<td>Time</td>
<td>Activity</td>
<td>Host / Expert Script + Notes</td>
</tr>
<tr color="purple_bg">
<td>0:00-0:10</td>
<td>Welcome + Energy</td>
<td>Music ON before people enter (Cholo). Matt on spotlight: "Cameras on. You did not just watch a course, you built a working agent. Tonight you see what is next." Get 1's in the chat, read names out loud.</td>
</tr>
<tr color="purple_bg">
<td>0:10-0:25</td>
<td>Challenge Recap + Graduation</td>
<td>Matt: celebrate what they built over 15 days. Name graduates. Show 2 or 3 member agents (with permission). [graduation stats: fill after graduation]</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>0:25-0:30</td>
<td>Set up the 3-agent format</td>
<td>Matt: "Three experts, three agents, built live. Keep your own agent open. Every time you see something you like, add it to yours."</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>0:30-1:05</td>
<td>Agent 1: The Research Agent</td>
<td>Expert 1 builds live on screen-share. Narrate every step. Matt prompts the chat: "Drop a fire emoji when you would use this." Pick 2 to 3 to react.</td>
</tr>
<tr color="orange_bg">
<td>1:05-1:10</td>
<td>STATE CHANGE</td>
<td>Matt: "Stand up. Music ON." Cholo plays a 2-min track. "You just saw a research agent built from scratch. The next one is even better."</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1:10-1:45</td>
<td>Agent 2: The Image-Gen Agent</td>
<td>Expert 2 builds live. Show one content idea turned into a ready-to-post visual. Audience reacts in chat.</td>
</tr>
<tr color="orange_bg">
<td>1:45-1:50</td>
<td>BREAK + Energy</td>
<td>Cholo music. Matt: "Quick break. Stretch. Cameras back on in 5." Reset the energy.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1:50-2:25</td>
<td>Agent 3: The Customer-Finder Agent</td>
<td>Expert 3 builds live. Show it surfacing real leads or conversations. "This is how you find customers without paying for ads."</td>
</tr>
<tr color="green_bg">
<td>2:25-2:40</td>
<td>THE WOW MOMENT: Member Spotlights</td>
<td>Matt: "5 volunteers, share your screen, show the agent you built. 60 seconds each." Pick diverse examples. "Give them love in the chat." Julia screenshots every one for marketing.</td>
</tr>
<tr color="green_bg">
<td>2:40-2:55</td>
<td>Competition + Winners</td>
<td>Jay confirms each winner is a challenge graduate first. Danny + Matt announce: Grand $500, Runner-Up $300, Honorable Mention $100, Community MVP $100. For each: show the agent, explain why they won, music, celebrate.</td>
</tr>
<tr color="purple_bg">
<td>2:55-3:00</td>
<td>The Handoff</td>
<td>Music low. Matt: "You walked in with one agent. You are leaving with three new blueprints and a sharper agent. But it only matters if you use it. So one assignment, from you to you: tomorrow morning, type today's idea and post what it gives you. Just one. That is how this becomes a habit. Thank you for building. Now go use it." Music up. Celebrate. End.</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Troubleshooting Playbook

<table fit-page-width="true" header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg">
<td>Problem</td>
<td>Solution</td>
<td>Who Handles</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>An expert's agent breaks live</td>
<td>Fall back to the pre-built working version (every expert has one ready). Matt covers with the chat while it is fixed.</td>
<td>Expert + Matt</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Claude hits rate limits mid-build</td>
<td>Switch to a lighter model, or use the pre-built version to keep moving. Do not stall the room.</td>
<td>Expert</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>A member cannot follow along</td>
<td>Remind them: tonight is watch-and-improve, not build-from-zero. Support DMs them the key step. The recording is available after.</td>
<td>Support</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Someone did not do the challenge</td>
<td>Welcome them. They watch and take the three blueprints. Point them to the next challenge.</td>
<td>Support</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Internet drops for an expert</td>
<td>Matt fills with chat or a member spotlight. The expert rejoins. Cholo has a backup ready.</td>
<td>Cholo + Matt</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Energy drops mid-session</td>
<td>Matt calls a surprise state change. "Everyone stand up RIGHT NOW." 2-min music. No warning needed.</td>
<td>Matt</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Tech fails (OBS, Zoom, screen share)</td>
<td>Backup laptop ready. Cholo is tech only, not presenting.</td>
<td>Cholo</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Team Roles During the Summit

### The 3 Experts

**Expert 1: The Research Agent (about 35 min, 0:30 to 1:05)**

- Builds live: an agent that finds competitor moves, trends, and content ideas automatically.
- Should be good at: research workflows in Claude, explaining clearly to beginners, live demos.
- Prepares: the agent pre-built and working (to rebuild a clean version live), a real example business, the build rehearsed to under 5 minutes.

**Expert 2: The Image-Gen Agent (about 35 min, 1:10 to 1:45)**

- Builds live: an agent that turns a content idea into ready-to-post visuals.
- Should be good at: AI image tools, on-brand visuals, fast clear demos.
- Prepares: the agent pre-built, an example brand, the image flow tested end to end.

**Expert 3: The Customer-Finder Agent (about 35 min, 1:50 to 2:25)**

- Builds live: an agent that finds customers and people to promote the product (Reddit, X, communities).
- Should be good at: outreach and lead workflows, sorting leads, accessible tools.
- Prepares: the agent pre-built, a real example, a safe live search ready.

Full per-expert briefs and FAQ: see Summit Guest-Experts.

<table header-row="true">
<tr>
<td>Time</td>
<td>Who</td>
<td>What</td>
</tr>

<tr><td>1:10-1:45</td><td>Expert 2</td><td>Image-Gen Agent built live.</td></tr>
<tr><td>1:45-1:50</td><td>Cholo/Matt</td><td>Break + energy reset.</td></tr>

### Ops Team

<table header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg"><td>Person</td><td>Role</td><td>What They Do</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Matt**</td><td>MC + Host + Energy</td><td>Opens. Runs all state changes (music, movement). Manages time, gives 2-min warnings. Hosts spotlights. Announces winners. Closes.</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Experts 1, 2, 3**</td><td>Build the agents</td><td>Each builds one agent live and narrates it. Names TBC (placeholders).</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Danny**</td><td>Judging + Winners</td><td>Owns judging. Announces winners with Matt.</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Jay**</td><td>Winner verification</td><td>Confirms each winner is a challenge graduate before the slide.</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Alexa**</td><td>Ops Lead</td><td>Coordinates the team, monitors flow, handles escalations.</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Support (Jiia, Angelo)**</td><td>Chat Support</td><td>Answer questions, surface spotlights, DM anyone lost. [ team to confirm names ]</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Julia**</td><td>Community + Screenshots</td><td>Screenshots member agents during spotlights for marketing. [ team to confirm ]</td></tr>
<tr><td>**Cholo**</td><td>Tech + Music</td><td>Music for state changes, OBS/display, timers, recording. Backup laptop ready.</td></tr>
</table>

<callout icon="🚨" color="red_bg">
	**Energy is the job.** Every 30 minutes, Matt comes in with music or a movement break. The first summit lost the room when teaching ran too long. This time we protect the energy. No single build over 35 minutes without a state change.
</callout>

---

## What Success Looks Like

Every attendee leaves with:

- **Three clear agent blueprints** (research, image, customer-finder) they saw built from scratch
- **At least one upgrade** they added to their own agent live
- The **winners announced** and celebrated, verified as real graduates
- One thing to **do tomorrow morning** (run their agent, post the idea)
- The feeling that **they built something real** (the most important one)

---

## Competition & Prizes

<table fit-page-width="true" header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg"><td>Prize</td><td>Amount</td><td>Criteria</td></tr>
<tr><td>Grand Prize</td><td>$500 in credits</td><td>Best overall agent, brief, and output quality</td></tr>
<tr><td>Runner-Up</td><td>$300 in credits</td><td>Second best overall</td></tr>
<tr><td>Honorable Mention</td><td>$100 in credits</td><td>Third, a strong agent worth recognizing</td></tr>
<tr><td>Community MVP</td><td>$100 in credits</td><td>Most helpful member, community-voted</td></tr>
</table>

**Judging:** Agent works 25% | Output quality 25% | Tailored to their business 25% | Creativity 15% | Community engagement 10%. $1,000 in Nas.com credits across 4 prizes. The Community MVP is voted by members; the other three are judged. Each winner verified as a challenge graduate (Jay) before the announcement. Must be present to win.

---

## Logistics

- **Dry run:** 30 minutes with the 3 experts before the event. Matt confirms the time.
- **300 person cap** (Zoom). Full room expected.
- **Record the session.** Send the replay to attendees after.
- **Matt hosts** everything outside the expert build segments.
- **Screens and pre-built agents ready** before the session starts.
- **Lock the start time in Source of Truth**, then the run-of-show is final.

---
*Pair with: Summit Guest-Experts (per-expert briefs), Source of Truth (the facts), and Competition Rules & Entry Process. Questions before the dry run come straight to Matt. AI SCHOOL. NAS.com. CONFIDENTIAL.*
